description_long: This is a stone sculpture depicting a seated female deity or figure, adorned with an elaborate headdress and jewelry. The figure sits cross-legged against a rounded, arched stone backdrop, holding a staff or scepter in her right hand and a small vessel in her left. The facial features are finely carved, with a serene expression and detailed hair. The sculpture is carved in high relief, with the figure projecting prominently from the flat backing. The back of the sculpture is smooth and unadorned, indicating it is meant to be viewed from the front. The overall craftsmanship suggests a traditional or religious context, with attention to anatomical and ornamental details.
description: A detailed stone sculpture of a seated female figure with a headdress, holding a staff and vessel, carved in high relief against a smooth arched backing.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the full figure, facial details, headdress, and both held objects clearly.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the depth of the relief and the projection of the figure from the backing.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the smooth, unadorned arched backing of the sculpture.
description_view_3: Right side view emphasizing the figure's profile and the three-dimensionality of the carving.
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate installation environment.
